REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT VIOLIE PASTOR'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO EXPLAIN THE INCONSISTENCY OF ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AND THE EXHIBIT TO COMPLAINT (COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THAT PARTIES ORALLY AGREED THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE 100% OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY VERSUS GRANT DEED REFLECTS THAT PARTIES "CONTINUE TO HOLD THE SAME PROPORTIONATE INTEREST" IN THE PROPERTY). THESE ALLEGATIONS ARE MATERIAL AND GO TO THE HEART OF THE DISPUTE - THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING STATUTE OF FRAUDS, WHICH WAS SATISFIED BY ALLEGATIONS OF DEBT PAYMENT AND REPLACEMENT PROPERTY SELECTION. DEMURRER TO OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION WAS OPPOSED AND NOT FURTHER PURSUED IN THE WRITTEN REPLY, UNLIKE THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses, Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Cross-complainant Violie D. Pastor's Motion To Compel Further Responses, Production Of Documents And For Sanctions is GRANTED.

The court construes Cross-complainant's motion to be presented pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.310: Subdivision (a) of section 2031.310 provides: "On receipt of a response to a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, the demanding party may move for an order compelling further response to the demand if the demanding party deems that any of the following apply: (1) A statement of compliance with the demand is incomplete. (2) A representation of inability to comply is inadequate, incomplete, or evasive. (3) An objection in the response is without merit or too general."

Cross-complainant has demonstrated reasonable meet and confer.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See CCP Section 2017.010.) Cross-complainant has shown Cross-defendant TA & TO Holdings, L.P.'s responses are incomplete/non-compliant and its objections are without merit. Cross-defendant has not demonstrated the merit of any cognizable objection.

Cross-defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. shall provide further responses in strict compliance with C.C.P. 2031.220 & 2031.230 to Document Demand Nos. 3, 6, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18, 19, 22, 24 & 25. The further responses shall be free of objections. For each category of documents to which Cross-defendant's further response suggests it is producing or has produced any responsive documents, the response shall identify what documents it is producing or has produced in response to that category. In response to Demand No. 13 the party verifying the further responses shall produce a legible California driver's license or government issued identification. Cross-defendant shall provide the further responses and production within 15 days of notice of this order.

The court does not find Cross-defendant acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Cross-defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. and counsel H. Douglas Daniel, and the Law Offices of H. Douglas Daniel, jointly and severally, shall pay $2,825 to Cross-complainant as sanctions, payment within 15 days of notice of this order

Cross-complainant is admonished that future motions that do not identify their statutory or common law basis in the notice of motion will likely be ordered off calendar.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanction
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT VIOLIE D. PASTOR's Motion For Terminating Sanction.

Defendant Violie D. Pastor's motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED.

Defendant's motion seeks terminating sanctions because cross-defendant Ta & To Holdings L.P. allegedly failed to comply with this court's February 27, 2025, order and provide discovery responses. The predicate for the motion is false as Cross-defendant provided the responses on March 18, 2025. (Daniel Decl., par. 4.) Despite receiving the responses and meeting and conferring with cross-defendant, Defendant kept this outdated motion on calendar. Defendant then argues in reply that the responses are insufficient, an issue that Cross-defendant has not had a chance to address. Defendant fails to show a willful violation.

Defendant did not act with substantial justification. (Lang v. Hochman (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 1225, 1244.) Defendant and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $250 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by April 28, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 4, 2025.

A proposed order is emailed to the court. Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
